reachability_artifacts: built sworn help, sworn account, and MCP tool-list output proving no managed inference purchase, balance, cache, proxy, or get_credits surface
built sworn run with an active account and a network sentinel, proving no account-credit request and no credits.json mutation

effort_complexity.rationale: See spec.json; the implementer must separate removed Sworn commerce from retained provider quota semantics across a broad file inventory.

release_benefit: A SwornAgent user can use account identity without losing provider credentials, changing model routes, purchasing managed inference, or generating unapproved outbound traffic.

positive_scenarios: Users continue receiving actionable provider quota errors without seeing Sworn credit commerce.
Account, TUI, MCP, and current docs expose only capabilities that exist.
negative_scenarios: No startup path can fetch or cache a Sworn credit balance.
No cleanup guard may erase historical release evidence or generic provider quota vocabulary.
validation.benefit_hypothesis: Removing nonexistent commerce and managed inference surfaces eliminates hidden spend expectations and misleading product claims.

design_decisions.choice: Remove Sworn-managed credit commerce and cache surfaces rather than repair their schemas.
design_decisions.human_decision: Ratified 2026-07-15: remove proxy, purchase, credit balance, cache, TUI, MCP, and active public contract surfaces.
design_decisions.rationale: Managed inference and its financial metering are outside the approved product boundary.

design_decisions.choice: Retain provider quota classification independently of Sworn commerce.
options: retain provider terminal classification with provider-only remediation
design_decisions.rationale: Provider quota exhaustion remains a real fail-fast condition even when Sworn sells no inference credits.
